results.description: Usage of old generation EC2 instances (e.g., t2, m3, c4, r3). Newer generations are often cheaper and faster.
results.remediation: Upgrade to current generation instances (e.g., t3, m5, c5, r5).

results.description: Usage of very large instance types (xlarge+). Ensure this capacity is actually needed.
results.remediation: Review utilization metrics. Consider rightsizing or Spot Instances.

results.description: EBS volume is not encrypted. This is a security risk and often indicates unmanaged infrastructure.
Usage of Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1/io2). These are very expensive.
results.remediation: Verify if gp3 can meet performance requirements at a lower cost.

results.description: No spot instances detected. Spot instances can save 50-90% on compute costs for interruptible workloads.
results.estimated_savings: 50-90% savings on compute (hundreds to thousands per month)
results.remediation: Use spot instances for batch jobs, data analysis, and optional tasks. Consider aws_spot_instance_request or spot_price in launch templates.

recommendations: 💰 Optimize 4 high-cost issues for significant savings
⚠️ Infrastructure needs improvement - consider professional review

top_issues.estimated_savings: 50-90% savings on compute (hundreds to thousands per month)
